title: Delhi World Championships: Engaging Gen Z and Alpha Fans
indian_angle: India is hosting the Badminton World Championships, making this a significant event for the nation's sporting landscape and fan engagement.
summary: The article discusses the need for badminton to attract younger generations, Gen Z and Gen Alpha, as India prepares to host the World Championships in Delhi.

title: BAI President Sarma on Monkeys at India Open
indian_angle: The President of the Badminton Association of India made a notable statement regarding an incident at the India Open, an important domestic event.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma commented on an incident involving monkeys at a badminton game, questioning the concern.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System for Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This marks a major change in scoring rules for all domestic badminton tournaments in India, starting with an event in Ernakulam.
summary: A new 3x15 scoring system will be implemented across all domestic badminton tournaments in India, with its debut scheduled for an event in Ernakulam.

title: Indian Shuttlers Urged to Develop Independent Thinking
indian_angle: The article offers crucial advice for Indian shuttlers to cultivate self-reliance and strategic thinking on court, especially without immediate coaching support.
summary: An opinion piece suggests that Indian badminton players need to improve their ability to adapt and win matches independently, without constant guidance from coaches.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian star Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing strong performance on the international stage.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth was defeated in the final of the US Open, ending his strong run at the tournament as a finalist.

title: Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a major upset by defeating the World No. 6 player at the US Open.
summary: Rounak Chouhan, an Indian shuttler and Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a stir at the US Open Super 300 by defeating the World No. 6, Chou Tien Chen.
